heading: Time clocks; restrictions
label: 6106 Time clocks; restrictions
text: A recording clock may not be used to record time of an employee of an Executive department in the District of Columbia, except that the Bureau of Engraving and Printing may use such recording clocks.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 27(14th par. on p. 864), .Feb. 24, 1899, ch. 187, § 130 Stat. 864The words “District of Columbia” are substituted for “Washington” as a clearer statement.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.
Amendments1982— substituted “District of Columbia, except that the Bureau of Engraving and Printing may use such recording clocks” for “District of Columbia”.Pub. L. 97–221

Effective Date of 1982 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–221, § 6(b)96 Stat. 234July 23, 1982“The amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall take effect . Section 5 of this Act [set out in the Termination Date of 1982 Amendment note under ] shall not apply to the amendment made by this section.”October 1, 1982section 6101 of this title
